title: phlomoides schugnanica
intro: Phlomoides schugnanica, also known as Schugnan phlomoides or Schugnan mint, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, particularly in the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region of Tajikistan. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the relief of digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote overall health. Phlomoides schugnanica is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
